Alright, welcome back to LeadGen Strategy. Alright, so there are three options that you can do to make money with this. If you are selling remotely to clients that are not in your area, as in not within driving distance to you, then you're going to be doing a 10 minute call script to set the next appointment, send them the application after you've done that, and then you will close them on high ticket personalized coaching at $2,000 for 16 weeks. Now, if you are selling people in your local market, the only difference here is that you're going to be targeting people in that market, which means when you're searching, using search tools, or when you're looking on Facebook, or you're doing hashtags on Instagram, you're going to narrow that to people in your local area. You can follow the same structure, have your 10 minute call script, and set the next appointment, and put it in person rather than over the phone, which will increase the likelihood that you close those people. So instead of closing one out of three, you'll be able to close two out of three, and you'll probably also get a slightly higher sure rate for an in-person appointment, because you'll be meeting them. So if you work out of a studio, or you're sub-leasing, or you have your own gym, this is a great way of doing it, because this will ultimately make you, it'll be easy to make more money this way, because there's just more inherent trust in a local market. But the same process more or less applies, it's just in person, so you'll have a 10 minute call script, you'll set the next appointment, at the end of that 10 minute call script, they'll apply between the time that they get off the phone with you, and the time they come in, and then when they come in, you'll close them on a high ticket coaching program, and you still want to close them on a remote training program, I want to be clear here, you don't want to trade your time for dollars, and if you do that, you can train them, and then check in with them every 14 days or so, in person, if it's still have all the margin of online training, and so that's one of the benefits of this, if you want to go even more high ticket, you can sell them in person, but I'd recommend going remote, since the model is more profitable, all right? Third, if you're a monster sales person, so if you're like already a super sales Jedi, and if you haven't sold to cold traffic before, don't consider yourself a monster sales person, but if you really close lots of people, you go to grocery stores and you can close people, you call a cold, ice coldly list, and you can close people on the phone, then you're a monster sales person, and so then you can cut a little bit straight to the chase, and sell it straight on that first phone call, instead of making a 10 minute call, just close them at the end of it. That's if you're really good, if you're not, then just follow the process that we have, and if you're starting out, just follow either number one or number two, all right? That being said, if you are a monster sales person, what we've seen is that $602,000 is really the highest front end impulse buy that you're going to be able to get on a first call without any kind of pre-framing, and so part of the benefit of having the two step is that you can ultimately sell for a higher ticket because you have a little bit more trust, and there's more time as a lap, so they inherently just believe you more, all right? So that is the three different options that you have for selling, and I think it's important to understand that from a lead generation strategy standpoint of what we're trying to accomplish, all right? So the first major thing that I wanted to cover is the Messenger Setter Script.
